content.claim: Church-maintained school enrollment grew fastest (2.5× to 5×) in small towns and villages 2010-2022; simultaneously, pupils in settlements with no remaining state-run primary school rose from 1% to 4%—a maintainer shift layered atop selection pressures.
content.context: Physical school-network capacity is currently sufficient to sustain early selection almost anywhere; the genuine town-by-town feasibility bottleneck is the shrinking and unevenly distributed child population, combined with a maintainer and admissions design that concentrates selection into a minority of settlements.
limitations: The v1 source string was not re-verified during migration.
content.population: Population described in the migrated claim
content.time_scope: As reported in the v1 source corpus
